name: Caribbean Community (CARICOM)

colors.symbolism: the upper field — the Caribbean Sea
the lower field — the deeper sea
the sun and the central logo

description: A field divided horizontally into light blue (upper) and dark blue (lower), charged in the centre with a yellow logo: a stylised circle broken by two narrow lines (representing the letter C and the unity of the member states).
history: The Caribbean Community (CARICOM) was established by the Treaty of Chaguaramas in 1973, replacing the earlier CARIFTA. The flag features the organisation's emblem on a blue gradient field representing the Caribbean Sea.
